//: Runnable Jira example. Copy this dir into your project, fill the YOUR_* placeholders,
//2: set JIRA_API_TOKEN (+ ingress secrets) in a .env beside it, then `agenthook start`.
//secrets: Secrets stay ${ENV} refs (resolved from the shell or a .env beside this file),
//secrets2: so this file is shareable. State lives centrally in ~/.agenthook/<name>.
name: myproject-jira
//fullAuto: false (safe default) = agents prompt for permission. true = --dangerously-skip-permissions: a verified webhook runs UNSANDBOXED code on the host. Opt in only on a trusted host or in a container. See docs/architecture.md#security-posture.

tracker.//: Jira Cloud. Auth is HTTP Basic (base64 email:token) — `email` is the username, NOT a secret.
tracker.//auth: A Jira API token is Basic-only; `Authorization: Bearer <token>` is rejected. See docs/jira-setup.md §1.
tracker.//projectKey: the project whose workflow statuses drive the pipeline
tracker.//pipeline: The execution model (required). Jira routes on an issue's STATUS (not gids):
tracker.//pipeline2: an issue ENTERING a step's sourceStatus fires it. On exit the agent writes a
tracker.//pipeline3: VERDICT and the receiver TRANSITIONS the issue: advance -> successStatus (= the
tracker.//pipeline4: next step's source, so the move triggers it); fail -> failureStatus; hold ->
tracker.//pipeline5: holdStatus (parked for a human); changes -> a prior step's source (the rework loop,
tracker.//pipeline6: capped by maxAttempts, default 3). Status names match case-insensitively and are
tracker.//pipeline7: placeholders below — rename them to YOUR board's columns.
tracker.//pipeline8: TRANSITIONS MUST EXIST: Jira has no set-status; a target with no workflow transition
tracker.//pipeline9: from the issue's current status is a logged no-op. See docs/jira-setup.md §2.

pipeline.//holdStatus: where a `hold` parks an issue waiting on a human answer

pipeline.//createsWorktree: the receiver owns one git worktree per issue, shared across its steps
pipeline.//maxAttempts: optional: cap on runs per issue before a `changes` loop into this step fails (default 3)

pipeline.//changes: a review `changes` verdict bounces back to the previous step (code) on the same branch/PR

pipeline.//done: Terminal stage. manual = no agent: entering it only runs system actions.
pipeline.//done2: drainWorktree removes the code step's worktree and clears the attempt counters.
pipeline.//done3: Bound to review's successStatus, so an issue reaching `Done` tears down + stops.

ingress.//: How the receiver is reachable. The Jira webhook URL is created BY HAND and can't rotate,
ingress.//stable: so Jira REQUIRES a STABLE ingress — an ngrok reserved `domain`, or a `hosted` URL.
ingress.//stable2: NEVER an ephemeral tunnel (its URL changes each restart, orphaning the webhook).
ingress.//domain: reserved domain = the stable URL Jira's by-hand webhook points at (required here)
ingress.//hosted: Or a hosted URL behind your own proxy: { "type": "hosted", "url": "https://hook.YOUR_COMPANY.com" }
